claims.claim: AI x crypto / decentralized AI and compute
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: US regulatory ambiguity as a headwind
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Structurally risk-on, multi-year long the space
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Newly vocal optimism about pro-crypto US political/regulatory appointments (David Sacks, CFTC chair Brian, Chairman McHenry) — a shift from passive concern to active celebration of regulatory tailwinds
Emphasis on cross-chain interoperability / composability and 'any asset on any chain' (Universal, LayerZero) as a distinct theme
Privacy / secure-hardware blockchains for confidential state (Seismic) as a new investment thesis
2025 prediction that formal verification will become increasingly important
notes: Excited sectors are strongly confirmed by concrete a16z investments and endorsements in the window: AI x crypto (Gensyn RL swarm, 'decentralize AI → verification', Halliday's 'internet of agents'), DePIN (Helium/Movistar), infra/L2s/ZK (Espresso, Eigen, Aleo, Syndicate, Universal on Base), and payments (Halliday Payments, LayerZero x PayPal, Universal as 'Circle for cryptoassets'). Risk-on is confirmed by a heavy cadence of new lead investments and broadly bullish tone. The regulatory concern is treated as confirmed because he repeatedly engages the topic, welcoming pro-crypto appointments as fixes to the ambiguity he flagged — same underlying stance, now optimistic about resolution. Low-float/high-FDV concern was never addressed (absent). Token arrays were empty in both prediction and reality, consistent with his sector/theme-level investing.
